name: Hacker News: Submit Link Post
description: Open the Hacker News submit form, fill a link post (title, URL, optional text), pause for explicit operator approval, and submit only after the operator clicks Continue. To dry-run (draft without posting), let the workflow stop at the approval gate by clicking Stop in the extension UI — the form stays filled in the browser tab.

help.summary: Submit a Hacker News link post with an explicit approval gate before the final POST. Click Stop at the gate to leave a draft instead of submitting.

parameters.name: post_title
parameters.description: Title of the HN post (HN truncates aggressively; keep it factual and under ~80 chars).
parameters.example: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Jensen Huang on TPUs and supply chains

parameters.name: post_url
parameters.description: Canonical URL to submit. Must be the original source — HN penalises duplicate or aggregator URLs.

parameters.name: post_text
parameters.description: Optional body text for the link post. Pass an empty string when you do not want any body text. Keep short and non-promotional.

examples.description: Submit a link post with no body text (operator approves at the gate)
examples.command: rzn-browser run hn submit-link-post --param post_title="Show HN: rzn-browser, an extension-driven automation runtime" --param post_url="https://github.com/example/rzn-browser" --param post_text=""

examples.description: Submit a link post with a short context blurb
examples.command: rzn-browser run hn submit-link-post --param post_title="Dwarkesh Patel interviews Jensen Huang on TPUs and supply chains" --param post_url="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hrbq66XqtCo" --param post_text="Covers TPU competition, chip policy, and why Nvidia has not become a hyperscaler."

examples.description: Dry-run: fill the form but do not post (click Stop in the extension UI when the approval gate appears)
examples.command: rzn-browser run hn submit-link-post --param post_title="Test post — DO NOT APPROVE" --param post_url="https://example.com" --param post_text=""

help.returns: title_length, url, and text_length captured at the approval gate; on submit, the workflow waits for the post to land but does not return the new HN item id (read it from the redirected tab if needed).
notes: Caller must already be logged into Hacker News in the active browser profile. The workflow does not handle login.
HN aggressively flags duplicate URL submissions. Search HN for the URL before submitting if there is any doubt.
The approval gate uses request_user_intervention with no timeout. The workflow blocks until the operator clicks Continue (submit) or Stop (leave as draft).

browser_automation.description: Workflow sequence executed via the browser extension for drafting a Hacker News link submission and sending it only after an explicit approval gate.

sequences.name: hn_submit_link_post
sequences.description: Navigate to submit page -> fill title/url/text -> verify draft -> approval gate -> submit

required_variables.name: post_text
required_variables.description: Optional short text field content. Pass an empty string when you do not want any body text.

steps.name: Navigate to the HN submit page
Allow the submit form to settle
Verify the draft has a title and URL
steps.script: const title = document.querySelector("input[name='title']"); const url = document.querySelector("input[name='url']"); const text = document.querySelector("textarea[name='text']"); const titleValue = title && 'value' in title ? String(title.value || '').trim() : ''; const urlValue = url && 'value' in url ? String(url.value || '').trim() : ''; const textValue = text && 'value' in text ? String(text.value || '').trim() : ''; if (!titleValue) { throw new Error('Title is empty'); } if (!urlValue) { throw new Error('URL is empty'); } return { title_length: titleValue.length, url: urlValue, text_length: textValue.length };

steps.name: Pause for approval before submitting the link post
steps.message: HN link submission draft is ready. Click Continue only if you want to post it. Click Stop to leave it as a draft.
steps.instructions: If the title feels promotional, the URL looks duplicated, or the text field is too bloated, click Stop instead of Continue.
